code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test21-MAR-2025Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, mild hyperchloremia, low CO₂, and significant proteinuria with glucosuria and bilirubinuria, but no hematuria or leukocyturia. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity and mild ketonuria. These findings suggest impaired renal function, likely acute or chronic kidney disease, with possible tubular dysfunction. No clear evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment of renal and hepatic status.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
